channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Robinsons Retail to Close All No Brand Stores in the Philippines by June 2026
item.description: Robinsons Retail Holdings Inc. (RRHI), part of the Gokongwei Group and the Philippines’ second-largest retail conglomerate, announced the closure of all 11 standalone No Brand stores in the country by...

item.title: Guidance for Councils on Designing Safer Streets for Women and Girls
item.description: Councils in England are set to receive new guidance on redesigning streets to make them safer for women and girls. Active Travel England (ATE) is developing this guidance, which aims to tackle systemi...

item.title: Trump discusses possible Iran-U.S. negotiations as tensions persist over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump revealed that Iran had offered what he described as a valuable 'present' connected to energy routes through the Strait of Hormuz, a move he presented as a sign of goodwill ...

item.title: Human Remains of Manitoba Soldier from WWI Identified After More Than 100 Years
item.description: The remains of Albert Henry Detmold, a Manitoba soldier killed during the First World War, have been identified after being uncovered during a construction project in Loos-en-Gohelle, France, in 2020....

item.title: India Faces Rising Obesity Crisis Despite Misconceptions as Lifestyle Issue
item.description: India is grappling with a serious obesity problem, where one in four Indians is overweight, yet many still view it merely as a lifestyle choice rather than a chronic disease. This was highlighted at a...

item.title: Mark Scheifele Guides Jets to 4-1 Victory Over Golden Knights
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets secured an impressive 4-1 victory over the Vegas Golden Knights, with forward Mark Scheifele playing a pivotal role in leading his team to the win. The match, held on March 24, 2026,...

item.title: Tanzania Plans Zero Waste Campaign for the Food Sector
item.description: Tanzania is preparing for the International Day of Zero Waste, with a special focus on reducing waste in the food sector. The National Environment Management Council (NEMC), in collaboration with the ...

item.title: Analysis: Trump’s 15-Point Iran Proposal Draws Doubts Amid Outdated Framework
item.description: Diplomats familiar with US-Iran negotiations believe that Donald Trump’s latest 15-point framework for peace with Iran largely repeats a plan originally proposed in May 2025. That plan, drafted by Tru...

item.title: Analyzing the Impacts of Social Media on Society and Democracy
item.description: This article discusses the complex effects of social media on public perception, civil liberties, and democracy. John Nery, in his column and podcast 'In the Public Square,' engages with Roger McNamee...

item.title: Veteran broadcaster Fred Salanga, known as ‘Aksyon Fred’, dies at 77 after battling cancer
item.description: Fred Salanga, the iconic radio broadcaster from Negros Occidental known for his show ‘Aksyon Fred’, passed away on March 23, 2026, at the age of 77. He died after a prolonged battle with lung cancer, ...

item.title: Kenya’s Growing Debt Concerns and the Struggle for Accountable Borrowing
item.description: The article explores Kenya’s rising political and economic debate over public debt management, sparked by constitutional lawyer Willis Evans Otieno’s campaign, “We Refuse to Pay What’s Unlawful.” Otie...

item.title: ABC Journalists Strike Over Pay, Demand Better Working Conditions Amid Middle East Crisis
item.description: Some of the ABC’s most well-known figures, such as David Marr and Fran Kelly, walked off the job on Wednesday, joining more than 2,000 staff on strike demanding higher pay and better working condition...

item.title: Pat Voss apologises to Harrison Petty after AFL taunt controversy
item.description: Fremantle forward Pat Voss has publicly apologised to Melbourne defender Harrison Petty for mocking him with a 'cry baby' gesture during Saturday night’s AFL match. Voss said he didn’t fully understan...

item.title: Mombasa Youth Mobilise to Promote Peaceful and Inclusive Politics Ahead of Elections
item.description: In Mombasa, a new wave of politically conscious youth has come together under the slogan 'Niko Kadi', meaning 'I am here', to push back against the divisive and toxic political culture that has long d...

item.title: Developments in Iran conflict: Casualties in Tehran and US-Iran statements
item.description: United States President Donald Trump asserted on Tuesday that his country has won the conflict in Iran, dismissing reports suggesting otherwise as 'fake news'. US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth claimed...

item.title: Essendon’s leadership struggles to admit the need for a rebuild despite clear signs on the field
item.description: The article examines the growing perception that the Essendon Football Club is in the midst of a rebuild, even though its leadership refuses to label it as such. Since Andrew Welsh became club preside...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens basketball with Kawe arena plans and Dar City BAL preparations
item.description: Tanzania's basketball scene is getting big support as Minister Paul Makonda confirmed government plans to build a modern multi-purpose basketball arena in Kawe. This project is part of wider efforts t...

item.title: Trump's $200 Billion Request Signals Major Escalation in Iran Conflict
item.description: The Trump administration has requested an additional $200 billion for its military operations against Iran, a move that underscores the scale of the U.S.'s growing involvement in the Middle East. This...

item.title: Delhi Court Sentences Kashmiri Separatist Asiya Andrabi to Life Imprisonment, Associates to 30 Years
item.description: In a significant ruling, a Delhi court on Tuesday awarded life imprisonment to Kashmiri separatist and Dukhtaran-e-Millat chief, Asiya Andrabi, for conspiring against the Indian State. The court also ...

item.title: 33 Bodies Found in Mass Grave in Kericho, Including 25 Children
item.description: A mass grave containing 33 bodies has been discovered in Kericho, with 25 children and 8 adults among the victims, as confirmed by Dr. Richard Njoroge, a pathologist leading the investigation. The exh...
